ogTitle: Clifton Beach vs Colebrook: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clifton Beach and Colebrook.
heading: Clifton Beach vs Colebrook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clifton Beach, Tasmania and Colebrook, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clifton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Colebrook (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clifton Beach or Colebrook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clifton Beach has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Colebrook scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clifton Beach scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Colebrook scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clifton Beach or Colebrook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clifton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
